strengths: Current role at re:cinq explicitly involves AI-agent infrastructure and MCP (Model Context Protocol) code generators supporting agentic workflows — a direct, rare match to the JD's AI agent development requirement.

Primary stack alignment is strong: TypeScript and Node.js at re:cinq, TypeScript/React/AWS CDK at Optilyz, with MongoDB, Docker, and Kubernetes covering the database and cloud/containerization expectations across real production contexts.

Career-long TDD discipline evidenced by practice across multiple roles, creation of open-source test runner (Trial) and assertion library (Fluent-Asserts), and community testing workshops — signals the code quality and testing-framework mindset the role demands.
concerns: Nest.js is a primary stack requirement with no explicit mention in the resume; Node.js experience transfers well but familiarity with Nest's opinionated DI, module, and decorator patterns is unverified.
Kotlin is listed as a secondary technology; while the candidate has some Java exposure from embedded and tooling work at Altom, the depth and recency are unclear.
Vitest and Playwright — the JD's specified testing frameworks — are not called out explicitly; strong general testing culture is evident but stack-specific framework experience should be confirmed.
response.justification: Bogdan brings over 15 years of fullstack engineering depth anchored in the JD's primary stack, and his current re:cinq role stands out for explicit AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling work — a differentiating requirement few candidates will satisfy at this level of specificity. His cloud, database, and containerization coverage (AWS CDK, MongoDB, Kubernetes, Docker) maps cleanly to the JD's infrastructure expectations, and his sustained TDD practice — backed by open-source test tooling and community teaching — signals the engineering discipline and code-quality orientation the role is built around. The Nest.js gap is the most substantive open question, but his Node.js architectural depth across high-throughput production systems makes it a learnable delta rather than a fundamental mismatch.
